title: andropogon hirtus subsp. podotrichus
intro: Andropogon hirtus subsp. podotrichus, commonly known as East Indian sandalwood, is a type of grass native to India and Sri Lanka. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of East Indian sandalwood include reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. East Indian sandalwood can be prepared into herbal teas, essential oils, and powders, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health issues.
